title: Receiver of an attachment in an automated email
excerpt:
Is there any way to avoid the receiver of an automated email to view/ download an attachment without having a monday account (guest or user)? 
I want them to be able to click on a link/ attachment and view it without any…

posts.author: Aleah (Aleah Mua'e)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there any way to avoid the receiver of an automated email to view/ download an attachment without having a monday account (guest or user)?
I want them to be able to click on a link/ attachment and view it without any extra steps.

posts.author: TimSburv (Timoti Sburv)
posts.cookedHtml:
Unfortunately, Monday doesn’t provide a direct way to share file attachments in automated emails without requiring the recipient to log in. If the file is stored in Monday, the default behavior restricts access to users or guests with permissions.

A workaround is to use an external file storage service like Google Drive, Dropbox, or OneDrive. Upload the file there, generate a shareable link with public or restricted access settings, and include that link in your automated email instead of attaching the file directly. This way, recipients can open the file without needing a Monday account.

Hope this helps!

posts.author: Aleah (Aleah Mua'e)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for taking the time to reply and explain!

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey, there is a native monday extension app that provides that functionality named Supermail.

auth.monday.com

SuperMail - monday.com Marketplace

Emails: Design, Send and Track directly from monday.com

It will allow u sending automated emails with attachments, also hyperlinks and to design your mails with rich text

posts.author: Aleah (Aleah Mua'e)
posts.cookedHtml: Wow, this looks great. Thank you so much!
